question: Which active organizations had more than one paid invoice?
gold_sql: SELECT o.name, COUNT(i.id) AS paid_invoice_count FROM organizations o JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'active' AND i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.name HAVING COUNT(i.id) > 1 ORDER BY paid_invoice_count DESC, o.name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.id, o.name FROM organizations AS o JOIN invoices AS i ON i.organization_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'active' AND i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.id, o.name HAVING COUNT(i.id) > 1 ORDER BY o.name ASC, o.id ASC

question: Which borrowers had an approved application but no declined applications?
gold_sql: SELECT b.full_name, COUNT(a.id) AS approved_applications FROM borrowers b JOIN applications a ON a.borrower_id = b.id WHERE a.application_status = 'approved' GROUP BY b.full_name HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN a.application_status = 'declined' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0 ORDER BY approved_applications DESC, b.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT b.full_name FROM borrowers b WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM applications a WHERE a.borrower_id = b.id AND a.application_status = 'approved') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM applications a WHERE a.borrowor_id = b.id AND a.application_status = 'declined') ORDER BY b.full_name ASC

question: Which departments hired at least one candidate?
gold_sql: SELECT d.department_name, COUNT(a.id) AS hires FROM departments d JOIN job_openings j ON j.department_id = d.id JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id WHERE a.application_status = 'hired' GROUP BY d.department_name HAVING COUNT(a.id) > 0 ORDER BY hires DESC, d.department_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT d.department_name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS hired_candidates FROM departments d JOIN job_openings j ON j.department_id = d.id JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id WHERE a.application_status = 'hired' GROUP BY d.department_name ORDER BY hired_candidates DESC, d.department_name ASC

question: Which origin warehouses have more than one shipment on active routes?
gold_sql: SELECT w.warehouse_name, COUNT(s.id) AS shipment_count FROM warehouses w JOIN routes r ON r.origin_warehouse_id = w.id JOIN shipments s ON s.route_id = r.id WHERE r.route_status = 'active' GROUP BY w.warehouse_name HAVING COUNT(s.id) > 1 ORDER BY shipment_count DESC, w.warehouse_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT w.warehouse_name, COUNT(s.id) AS shipment_count FROM warehouses w JOIN routes r ON r.origin_warehouse_id = w.id JOIN shipments s ON s.route_id = r.id WHERE r.route_status = 'active' GROUP BY w.warehouse_name HAVING COUNT(s.id) > 1 ORDER BY shipment_count DESC, w.warehouse_name ASC

question: Which patients have an active prescription?
gold_sql: SELECT pa.full_name, COUNT(pr.id) AS active_prescriptions FROM patients pa JOIN appointments a ON a.patient_id = pa.id JOIN prescriptions pr ON pr.appointment_id = a.id WHERE pr.prescription_status = 'active' GROUP BY pa.full_name ORDER BY active_prescriptions DESC, pa.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.full_name FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON a.patient_id = p.id JOIN prescriptions pr ON pr.appointment_id = a.id WHERE pr.prescription_status = 'active' ORDER BY p.full_name ASC

question: Which channels produced at least one qualified lead?
gold_sql: SELECT c.channel, COUNT(l.id) AS qualified_leads FROM campaigns c JOIN leads l ON l.campaign_id = c.id WHERE l.lead_status = 'qualified' GROUP BY c.channel HAVING COUNT(l.id) > 0 ORDER BY qualified_leads DESC, c.channel ASC
pred_sql: SELECT c.channel, COUNT(l.id) AS qualified_lead_count FROM campaigns c JOIN leads l ON l.campaign_id = c.id WHERE l.lead_status = 'qualified' GROUP BY c.channel ORDER BY qualified_lead_count DESC, c.channel ASC

question: Which guests had total folio spend above 700 dollars?
gold_sql: SELECT g.full_name, SUM(f.total_amount_usd) AS total_spend_usd FROM guests g JOIN reservations r ON r.guest_id = g.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = r.id GROUP BY g.full_name HAVING SUM(f.total_amount_usd) > 700 ORDER BY total_spend_usd DESC, g.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT g.full_name, SUM(f.total_amount_usd) AS total_spend FROM guests g JOIN reservations r ON r.guest_id = g.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = r.id GROUP BY g.full_name HAVING SUM(f.total_amount_usd) > 700 ORDER BY total_spend DESC, g.full_name ASC

question: Which assets have at least one open vulnerability?
gold_sql: SELECT a.asset_name, COUNT(v.id) AS open_vulnerabilities FROM assets a JOIN vulnerabilities v ON v.asset_id = a.id WHERE v.vuln_status = 'open' GROUP BY a.asset_name HAVING COUNT(v.id) > 0 ORDER BY open_vulnerabilities DESC, a.asset_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT a.asset_name FROM assets a JOIN vulnerabilities v ON v.asset_id = a.id WHERE v.vuln_status != 'remediated' GROUP BY a.id, a.asset_name HAVING COUNT(v.id) >= 1 ORDER BY a.asset_name ASC

question: Which work orders failed quality checks?
gold_sql: SELECT w.work_order_code, COUNT(q.id) AS failed_checks FROM work_orders w JOIN quality_checks q ON q.work_order_id = w.id WHERE q.quality_status = 'failed' GROUP BY w.work_order_code ORDER BY failed_checks DESC, w.work_order_code ASC
pred_sql: SELECT wo.work_order_code, qc.defect_count FROM work_orders wo JOIN quality_checks qc ON qc.work_order_id = wo.id WHERE qc.quality_status = 'failed' ORDER BY qc.checked_at DESC

question: Which organizations have paid revenue above the average paid revenue of organizations in the same region?
gold_sql: WITH org_paid_revenue AS ( SELECT o.id, o.name, o.region, COALESCE(SUM(i.total_amount_usd), 0) AS paid_revenue_usd FROM organizations o LEFT JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id AND i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.id, o.name, o.region ), region_avg_revenue AS ( SELECT region, AVG(paid_revenue_usd) AS avg_paid_revenue_usd FROM org_paid_revenue GROUP BY region ) SELECT opr.id, opr.name, opr.region, opr.paid_revenue_usd, rar.avg_paid_revenue_usd FROM org_paid_revenue opr JOIN region_avg_revenue rar ON rar.region = opr.region WHERE opr.paid_revenue_usd > rar.avg_paid_revenue_usd ORDER BY opr.region ASC, opr.paid_revenue_usd DESC, opr.name ASC, opr.id ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.name AS organization_name, SUM(i.total_amount_usd) AS paid_revenue_usd FROM organizations o JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id WHERE i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.id, o.name HAVING SUM(i.total_amount_usd) > (SELECT AVG(SUM(i2.total_amount_usd)) AS avg_paid_revenue_usd FROM organizations o2 JOIN invoices i2 ON i2.organization_id = o2.id WHERE i2.status = 'paid' AND o2.region = o.region GROUP BY o2.id) ORDER BY paid_revenue_usd DESC, organization_name ASC

question: Which organizations have a largest invoice total greater than their own average invoice total?
gold_sql: WITH org_invoice_stats AS (SELECT o.id, o.name AS organization_name, MAX(i.total_amount_usd) AS largest_invoice_total_usd, AVG(i.total_amount_usd) AS average_invoice_total_usd FROM organizations o JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id, o.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 AND MAX(i.total_amount_usd) > AVG(i.total_amount_usd)) SELECT organization_name, largest_invoice_total_usd, average_invoice_total_usd FROM org_invoice_stats ORDER BY (largest_invoice_total_usd - average_invoice_total_usd) DESC, organization_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.name AS organization_name, MAX(i.total_amount_usd) AS max_invoice_total, AVG(i.total_amount_usd) AS avg_invoice_total FROM organizations o JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id, o.name HAVING MAX(i.total_amount_usd) > AVG(i.total_amount_usd) ORDER BY o.name ASC

question: For each industry, which organization has the highest total paid invoice revenue? If there is a tie, choose the organization whose name comes first alphabetically. Return the industry, organization name, and paid invoice revenue.
gold_sql: WITH org_revenue AS ( SELECT o.industry, o.id AS organization_id, o.name AS organization_name, COALESCE(SUM(i.total_amount_usd), 0) AS paid_invoice_revenue FROM organizations o LEFT JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id AND i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.industry, o.id, o.name ), ranked AS ( SELECT industry, organization_name, paid_invoice_revenue, ROW_NUMBER() OVER ( PARTITION BY industry ORDER BY paid_invoice_revenue DESC, organization_name ASC, organization_id ASC ) AS rn FROM org_revenue ) SELECT industry, organization_name, paid_invoice_revenue FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1 ORDER BY industry, organization_name
pred_sql: SELECT o.industry, o.name AS organization_name, SUM(i.total_amount_usd) AS paid_invoice_revenue FROM organizations o JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id WHERE i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.id, o.industry, o.name ORDER BY paid_invoice_revenue DESC, o.name ASC, o.industry ASC LIMIT 10

question: Which organizations had a higher total of billable units in their most recent usage month than in the month immediately before it? Return the organization name and both monthly totals, ordered by organization name.
gold_sql: WITH monthly_usage AS ( SELECT organization_id, strftime('%Y-%m', event_timestamp) AS usage_month, SUM(billable_units) AS billable_units FROM usage_events GROUP BY organization_id, strftime('%Y-%m', event_timestamp) ), ranked AS ( SELECT organization_id, usage_month, billable_units, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY organization_id ORDER BY usage_month DESC) AS month_rank FROM monthly_usage ), latest AS ( SELECT organization_id, usage_month AS latest_month, billable_units AS latest_billable_units FROM ranked WHERE month_rank = 1 ), prior AS ( SELECT organization_id, usage_month AS prior_month, billable_units AS prior_billable_units FROM ranked WHERE month_rank = 2 ) SELECT o.name AS organization_name, latest.latest_month, latest.latest_billable_units, prior.prior_month, prior.prior_billable_units FROM latest JOIN prior ON prior.organization_id = latest.organization_id JOIN organizations o ON o.id = latest.organization_id WHERE latest.latest_billable_units > prior.prior_billable_units ORDER BY o.name
pred_sql: SELECT o.name AS organization_name, MAX(CASE WHEN uem.event_timestamp >= DATE('now', '-1 month') THEN SUM(uem.billable_units) END) AS recent_month_billable_units, MAX(CASE WHEN uem.event_timestamp < DATE('now', '-1 month') AND uem.event_timestamp >= DATE('now', '-2 months') THEN SUM(uem.billable_units) END) AS previous_month_billable_units FROM organizations o LEFT JOIN usage_events uem ON uem.organization_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id, o.name HAVING MAX(CASE WHEN uem.event_timestamp >= DATE('now', '-1 month') THEN SUM(uem.billable_units) END) > MAX(CASE WHEN uem.event_timestamp < DATE('now', '-1 month') AND uem.event_timestamp >= DATE('now', '-2 months') THEN SUM(uem.billable_units) END) ORDER BY o.name ASC

question: Which organizations have at least two distinct line-item descriptions across their paid invoices, and how many paid invoices and distinct descriptions do they have?
gold_sql: SELECT o.name, COUNT(DISTINCT i.id) AS paid_invoice_count, COUNT(DISTINCT ili.description) AS distinct_line_item_descriptions FROM organizations o JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id JOIN invoice_line_items ili ON ili.invoice_id = i.id WHERE i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.id, o.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ili.description) >= 2 ORDER BY distinct_line_item_descriptions DESC, paid_invoice_count DESC, o.name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.id AS organization_id, o.name AS organization_name, COUNT(DISTINCT i.id) AS paid_invoice_count, COUNT(DISTINCT ili.description) AS distinct_description_count FROM organizations o JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id LEFT JOIN invoice_line_items ili ON ili.invoice_id = i.id WHERE i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.id, o.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ili.description) >= 2 ORDER BY distinct_description_count DESC, organization_name ASC, organization_id ASC

question: Which industries have an average paid invoice revenue per organization above the overall average paid invoice revenue per organization across all organizations? Return the industry and the average paid revenue per organization, ordered from highest to lowest average.
gold_sql: WITH org_paid_revenue AS ( SELECT o.id, o.industry, COALESCE(SUM(i.total_amount_usd), 0) AS paid_revenue_usd FROM organizations o LEFT JOIN invoices i ON i.organization_id = o.id AND i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.id, o.industry ), industry_avg AS ( SELECT industry, AVG(paid_revenue_usd) AS avg_paid_revenue_per_org FROM org_paid_revenue GROUP BY industry ) SELECT industry, avg_paid_revenue_per_org FROM industry_avg WHERE avg_paid_revenue_per_org > (SELECT AVG(paid_revenue_usd) FROM org_paid_revenue) ORDER BY avg_paid_revenue_per_org DESC, industry ASC
pred_sql: WITH paid_invoice_revenue_per_org AS ( SELECT o.industry, i.organization_id, SUM(i.total_amount_usd) AS paid_invoice_revenue_usd FROM invoices i JOIN organizations o ON o.id = i.organization_id WHERE i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.industry, i.organization_id ), avg_paid_invoice_revenue_per_org AS ( SELECT AVG(paid_invoice_revenue_usd) AS avg_paid_invoice_revenue_usd FROM paid_invoice_revenue_per_org ) SELECT prpo.industry, AVG(prpo.paid_invoice_revenue_usd) AS avg_paid_invoice_revenue_usd FROM paid_invoice_revenue_per_org prpo GROUP BY prpo.industry HAVING AVG(prpo.paid_invoice_revenue_usd) > (SELECT avg_paid_invoice_revenue_usd FROM avg_paid_invoice_revenue_per_org) ORDER BY avg_paid_invoice_revenue_usd DESC, prpo.industry ASC

question: Among active organizations that have at least one paid invoice, which organization has the highest paid invoice revenue per seat on its most recent subscription? Return the organization name and the ratio rounded to two decimals, ordered by ratio descending and then name ascending.
gold_sql: WITH latest_subscriptions AS ( SELECT s.organization_id, s.seats, s.started_at, s.id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.organization_id ORDER BY s.started_at DESC, s.id DESC) AS rn FROM subscriptions s ), paid_revenue AS ( SELECT i.organization_id, SUM(i.total_amount_usd) AS paid_revenue_usd FROM invoices i WHERE i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY i.organization_id ), scored AS ( SELECT o.name AS organization_name, pr.paid_revenue_usd, ls.seats, ROUND(pr.paid_revenue_usd * 1.0 / NULLIF(ls.seats, 0), 2) AS revenue_per_seat FROM organizations o JOIN latest_subscriptions ls ON ls.organization_id = o.id AND ls.rn = 1 JOIN paid_revenue pr ON pr.organization_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'active' ) SELECT organization_name, revenue_per_seat FROM scored ORDER BY revenue_per_seat DESC, organization_name ASC LIMIT 1
pred_sql: SELECT o.name AS organization_name, ROUND(SUM(i.total_amount_usd) / NULLIF(s.seats, 0), 2) AS ratio FROM organizations o JOIN subscriptions s ON s.organization_id = o.id AND s.status = 'active' JOIN invoices i ON i.subscription_id = s.id AND i.status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.id, o.name, s.seats ORDER BY ratio DESC, o.name ASC LIMIT 14

question: For each asset type, how many distinct assets have both failed authentications and at least one alert?
gold_sql: WITH qualifying_assets AS (SELECT a.id, a.asset_type FROM assets a WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM auth_events ae WHERE ae.asset_id = a.id AND ae.auth_result = 'failure') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM alerts al WHERE al.asset_id = a.id)) SELECT asset_type, COUNT(*) AS distinct_assets_with_failures_and_alerts FROM qualifying_assets GROUP BY asset_type ORDER BY distinct_assets_with_failures_and_alerts DESC, asset_type ASC
pred_sql: SELECT a.asset_type, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count FROM assets a JOIN auth_events ae ON ae.asset_id = a.id JOIN alerts al ON al.asset_id = a.id GROUP BY a.asset_type HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ae.id) > 0 AND COUNT(DISTINCT al.id) > 0 ORDER BY asset_count DESC, a.asset_type ASC

question: Which organizations have more open vulnerabilities than completed patch jobs?
gold_sql: SELECT o.org_name, COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) AS open_vulnerability_count, COUNT(DISTINCT pj.id) AS completed_patch_job_count FROM organizations o LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.organization_id = o.id LEFT JOIN vulnerabilities v ON v.asset_id = a.id AND v.vuln_status = 'open' LEFT JOIN patch_jobs pj ON pj.asset_id = a.id AND pj.patch_status = 'completed' GROUP BY o.id, o.org_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) > COUNT(DISTINCT pj.id) ORDER BY open_vulnerability_count DESC, o.org_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.org_name, COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) AS open_vulns, COUNT(DISTINCT pj.id) AS completed_patch_jobs FROM organizations o JOIN assets a ON a.organization_id = o.id LEFT JOIN vulnerabilities v ON v.asset_id = a.id AND v.vuln_status = 'open' LEFT JOIN patch_jobs pj ON pj.asset_id = a.id AND pj.patch_status = 'completed' GROUP BY o.id, o.org_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) > COUNT(DISTINCT pj.id) ORDER BY open_vulns DESC, o.org_name ASC

question: Which organizations have at least one open incident but no completed patch jobs on any asset?
gold_sql: SELECT o.org_name FROM organizations o WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM incidents i WHERE i.organization_id = o.id AND i.incident_status = 'open') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assets a JOIN patch_jobs pj ON pj.asset_id = a.id WHERE a.organization_id = o.id AND pj.completed_at IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY o.org_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.org_name FROM organizations o JOIN incidents i ON i.organization_id = o.id LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.organization_id = o.id LEFT JOIN patch_jobs pj ON pj.asset_id = a.id AND pj.patch_status = 'completed' WHERE i.incident_status = 'open' GROUP BY o.id, o.org_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pj.id) = 0 ORDER BY o.org_name ASC

question: Which assets have the highest total security signals from alerts, failed logins, and open vulnerabilities?
gold_sql: WITH alert_counts AS ( SELECT asset_id, COUNT(*) AS alert_count FROM alerts GROUP BY asset_id ), failed_login_counts AS ( SELECT asset_id, COUNT(*) AS failed_login_count FROM auth_events WHERE auth_result = 'failure' GROUP BY asset_id ), open_vuln_counts AS ( SELECT asset_id, COUNT(*) AS open_vuln_count FROM vulnerabilities WHERE vuln_status = 'open' GROUP BY asset_id ) SELECT a.asset_name, COALESCE(ac.alert_count, 0) + COALESCE(fc.failed_login_count, 0) + COALESCE(ov.open_vuln_count, 0) AS total_security_signals FROM assets a LEFT JOIN alert_counts ac ON ac.asset_id = a.id LEFT JOIN failed_login_counts fc ON fc.asset_id = a.id LEFT JOIN open_vuln_counts ov ON ov.asset_id = a.id ORDER BY total_security_signals DESC, a.asset_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT a.asset_name, COUNT(*) AS total_signals FROM assets a LEFT JOIN alerts al ON al.asset_id = a.id AND al.alert_status = 'open' LEFT JOIN auth_events ae ON ae.asset_id = a.id AND ae.auth_result = 'failure' LEFT JOIN vulnerabilities v ON v.asset_id = a.id AND v.vuln_status = 'open' GROUP BY a.id, a.asset_name ORDER BY total_signals DESC, a.asset_name ASC

question: For each detection severity, how many linked incidents are currently open?
gold_sql: SELECT d.severity, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN i.incident_status = 'open' THEN i.id END) AS open_linked_incidents FROM detections d LEFT JOIN alerts a ON a.detection_id = d.id LEFT JOIN incident_alerts ia ON ia.alert_id = a.id LEFT JOIN incidents i ON i.id = ia.incident_id GROUP BY d.severity ORDER BY open_linked_incidents DESC, d.severity ASC
pred_sql: SELECT d.severity, COUNT(i.id) AS open_incident_count FROM detections d LEFT JOIN alerts a ON a.detection_id = d.id LEFT JOIN incident_alerts ia ON ia.alert_id = a.id LEFT JOIN incidents i ON i.id = ia.incident_id AND i.incident_status = 'open' GROUP BY d.severity ORDER BY open_incident_count DESC, d.severity ASC

question: For each owner team, how many distinct critical assets have either an open alert or an open vulnerability?
gold_sql: SELECT ao.owner_team, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS critical_asset_count FROM asset_owners ao JOIN assets a ON a.id = ao.asset_id WHERE a.criticality = 'critical' AND ( EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM alerts al WHERE al.asset_id = a.id AND al.alert_status = 'open') OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM vulnerabilities v WHERE v.asset_id = a.id AND v.vuln_status = 'open') ) GROUP BY ao.owner_team ORDER BY critical_asset_count DESC, ao.owner_team ASC
pred_sql: SELECT ao.owner_team, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS critical_assets FROM asset_owners ao JOIN assets a ON a.id = ao.asset_id LEFT JOIN alerts al ON al.asset_id = a.id AND al.alert_status = 'open' LEFT JOIN vulnerabilities v ON v.asset_id = a.id AND v.vuln_status != 'remediated' WHERE a.criticality = 'critical' GROUP BY ao.owner_team ORDER BY critical_assets DESC, ao.owner_team ASC

question: Which organizations have at least one active enrollment in every course they offer?
gold_sql: SELECT o.org_name FROM organizations o WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM instructors i JOIN courses c ON c.instructor_id = i.id WHERE i.organization_id = o.id) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM instructors i JOIN courses c ON c.instructor_id = i.id WHERE i.organization_id = o.id AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM enrollments e WHERE e.course_id = c.id AND e.enrollment_status = 'active')) ORDER BY o.org_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.org_name FROM organizations o WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM courses c JOIN instructors i ON i.id = c.instructor_id WHERE i.organization_id = o.id AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM enrollments e WHERE e.course_id = c.id AND e.enrollment_status = 'active')) ORDER BY o.org_name ASC

question: Which learners have a higher average score than the overall average for their organization?
gold_sql: SELECT l.full_name, AVG(aa.score) AS learner_average_score FROM learners l JOIN assessment_attempts aa ON aa.learner_id = l.id GROUP BY l.id, l.full_name, l.organization_id HAVING AVG(aa.score) > (SELECT AVG(aa2.score) FROM learners l2 JOIN assessment_attempts aa2 ON aa2.learner_id = l2.id WHERE l2.organization_id = l.organization_id) ORDER BY learner_average_score DESC, l.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT l.full_name, AVG(aa.score) AS avg_score FROM learners l JOIN assessment_attempts aa ON aa.learner_id = l.id WHERE aa.attempt_status = 'passed' GROUP BY l.id, l.full_name HAVING AVG(aa.score) > (SELECT AVG(aa2.score) FROM assessment_attempts aa2 WHERE aa2.learner_id IN (SELECT id FROM learners WHERE organization_id = l.organization_id)) ORDER BY avg_score DESC, l.full_name ASC

question: Which courses have the highest average score among courses with at least one active enrollment?
gold_sql: SELECT c.course_title, AVG(aa.score) AS average_score FROM courses c JOIN enrollments e ON e.course_id = c.id AND e.enrollment_status = 'active' JOIN assessments a ON a.course_id = c.id JOIN assessment_attempts aa ON aa.assessment_id = a.id GROUP BY c.id, c.course_title ORDER BY average_score DESC, c.course_title ASC
pred_sql: SELECT c.course_title, AVG(aa.score) AS avg_score FROM courses c JOIN enrollments e ON e.course_id = c.id JOIN assessment_attempts aa ON aa.learner_id = e.learner_id WHERE e.enrollment_status = 'active' GROUP BY c.id, c.course_title HAVING COUNT(aa.id) > 0 ORDER BY avg_score DESC, c.course_title ASC

question: Which learners are enrolled only in courses whose assessments they passed?
gold_sql: SELECT l.full_name FROM learners l WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM enrollments e WHERE e.learner_id = l.id) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM enrollments e JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id JOIN assessments a ON a.course_id = c.id WHERE e.learner_id = l.id AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assessment_attempts aa WHERE aa.learner_id = l.id AND aa.assessment_id = a.id AND aa.attempt_status = 'passed')) ORDER BY l.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT DISTINCT l.full_name FROM learners l JOIN enrollments e ON e.learner_id = l.id JOIN courses c ON c.id = e.course_id JOIN assessments a ON a.course_id = c.id JOIN assessment_attempts aa ON aa.learner_id = l.id AND aa.assessment_id = a.id WHERE aa.attempt_status = 'passed' ORDER BY l.full_name ASC

question: Which organizations have the highest average score normalized by assessment max score?
gold_sql: SELECT o.org_name, AVG(aa.score * 1.0 / a.max_score) AS normalized_average_score FROM organizations o JOIN learners l ON l.organization_id = o.id JOIN assessment_attempts aa ON aa.learner_id = l.id JOIN assessments a ON a.id = aa.assessment_id GROUP BY o.id, o.org_name ORDER BY normalized_average_score DESC, o.org_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.org_name, AVG(aa.score * 1.0 / a.max_score) AS avg_normalized_score FROM organizations o JOIN learners l ON l.organization_id = o.id JOIN assessment_attempts aa ON aa.learner_id = l.id JOIN assessments a ON a.id = aa.assessment_id WHERE a.max_score > 0 GROUP BY o.id, o.org_name ORDER BY avg_normalized_score DESC, o.org_name ASC

question: Which learners achieved the top score in every assessment they attempted?
gold_sql: SELECT l.full_name FROM learners l WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assessment_attempts aa WHERE aa.learner_id = l.id) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM assessment_attempts aa WHERE aa.learner_id = l.id AND aa.score < (SELECT MAX(aa2.score) FROM assessment_attempts aa2 WHERE aa2.assessment_id = aa.assessment_id)) ORDER BY l.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT l.full_name FROM learners l JOIN assessment_attempts aa ON aa.learner_id = l.id GROUP BY l.id, l.full_name HAVING COUNT(*) = SUM(CASE WHEN aa.score = (SELECT MAX(aa2.score) FROM assessment_attempts aa2 WHERE aa2.assessment_id = aa.assessment_id) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ORDER BY l.full_name ASC

question: Which organizations have alerts driven by more than one detection tactic across their assets?
gold_sql: SELECT o.org_name, COUNT(DISTINCT d.tactic) AS tactic_count FROM organizations o JOIN assets a ON a.organization_id = o.id JOIN alerts al ON al.asset_id = a.id JOIN detections d ON d.id = al.detection_id GROUP BY o.id, o.org_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT d.tactic) > 1 ORDER BY tactic_count DESC, o.org_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.org_name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.detection_id) AS distinct_tactics FROM organizations o JOIN assets a ON a.organization_id = o.id JOIN alerts al ON al.asset_id = a.id GROUP BY o.id, o.org_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.detection_id) > 1 ORDER BY o.org_name ASC

question: Which assets have more open vulnerabilities than completed patch jobs?
gold_sql: WITH vuln_counts AS (SELECT asset_id, COUNT(*) AS open_vulnerability_count FROM vulnerabilities WHERE vuln_status = 'open' GROUP BY asset_id), patch_counts AS (SELECT asset_id, COUNT(*) AS completed_patch_job_count FROM patch_jobs WHERE patch_status = 'completed' GROUP BY asset_id) SELECT a.asset_name, COALESCE(vc.open_vulnerability_count, 0) AS open_vulnerability_count, COALESCE(pc.completed_patch_job_count, 0) AS completed_patch_job_count FROM assets a LEFT JOIN vuln_counts vc ON vc.asset_id = a.id LEFT JOIN patch_counts pc ON pc.asset_id = a.id WHERE COALESCE(vc.open_vulnerability_count, 0) > COALESCE(pc.completed_patch_job_count, 0) ORDER BY open_vulnerability_count DESC, completed_patch_job_count ASC, a.asset_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT a.asset_name, COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) AS open_vulns, COUNT(DISTINCT pj.id) AS completed_patch_jobs FROM assets a JOIN vulnerabilities v ON v.asset_id = a.id AND v.vuln_status = 'open' LEFT JOIN patch_jobs pj ON pj.asset_id = a.id AND pj.patch_status = 'completed' GROUP BY a.id, a.asset_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) > COUNT(DISTINCT pj.id) ORDER BY open_vulns DESC, a.asset_name ASC

question: Which organization has how many distinct assets with no alerts and no open vulnerabilities?
gold_sql: SELECT o.org_name, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM alerts al WHERE al.asset_id = a.id) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM vulnerabilities v WHERE v.asset_id = a.id AND v.vuln_status = 'open') THEN a.id END) AS assets_with_no_alerts_and_no_open_vulns FROM organizations o LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.organization_id = o.id GROUP BY o.org_name ORDER BY assets_with_no_alerts_and_no_open_vulns DESC, o.org_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.org_name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count FROM organizations o JOIN assets a ON a.organization_id = o.id WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM alerts al WHERE al.asset_id = a.id) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM vulnerabilities v WHERE v.asset_id = a.id AND v.vuln_status != 'remediated') GROUP BY o.id, o.org_name ORDER BY asset_count DESC, o.org_name ASC

question: Which actor emails recorded failed logins on assets that belong to organizations with open incidents, and how many failed logins did each email record?
gold_sql: SELECT ae.actor_email, COUNT(*) AS failed_login_count FROM auth_events ae JOIN assets a ON a.id = ae.asset_id WHERE ae.auth_result = 'failure' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM incidents i WHERE i.organization_id = a.organization_id AND i.incident_status = 'open') GROUP BY ae.actor_email ORDER BY failed_login_count DESC, ae.actor_email ASC
pred_sql: SELECT ae.actor_email, COUNT(ae.id) AS failed_login_count FROM auth_events ae JOIN assets a ON a.id = ae.asset_id JOIN organizations o ON o.id = a.organization_id JOIN incidents i ON i.organization_id = o.id WHERE ae.auth_result = 'failure' AND i.incident_status = 'open' GROUP BY ae.actor_email ORDER BY failed_login_count DESC, ae.actor_email ASC

question: Which borrowers have a perfect repayment record with no missed payments?
gold_sql: SELECT b.full_name, COUNT(r.id) AS total_repayments, SUM(CASE WHEN r.repayment_status = 'paid' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS paid_count FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id JOIN repayments r ON r.loan_id = l.id GROUP BY b.full_name HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN r.repayment_status = 'missed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0 ORDER BY total_repayments DESC, b.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT b.full_name FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id JOIN repayments r ON r.loan_id = l.id GROUP BY b.full_name HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN r.repayment_status = 'missed' THEN 1 END) = 0 ORDER BY b.full_name ASC

question: For each state, find the borrower with the highest total loan principal.
gold_sql: SELECT state, full_name, total_principal_usd FROM (SELECT b.state, b.full_name, SUM(l.principal_usd) AS total_principal_usd, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY b.state ORDER BY SUM(l.principal_usd) DESC) AS rnk FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id GROUP BY b.state, b.full_name) ranked WHERE rnk = 1 ORDER BY state ASC, full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT b.state, b.full_name, SUM(l.principal_usd) AS total_principal FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id GROUP BY b.state, b.full_name ORDER BY total_principal DESC, b.state ASC, b.full_name ASC LIMIT 10

question: Which borrowers have total loan principal above their state average?
gold_sql: SELECT b.full_name, b.state, SUM(l.principal_usd) AS total_principal_usd FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id GROUP BY b.full_name, b.state HAVING SUM(l.principal_usd) > (SELECT AVG(sub_total) FROM (SELECT SUM(l2.principal_usd) AS sub_total FROM borrowers b2 JOIN loans l2 ON l2.borrower_id = b2.id WHERE b2.state = b.state GROUP BY b2.id)) ORDER BY total_principal_usd DESC, b.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT b.full_name, b.state, SUM(l.principal_usd) AS total_principal FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id GROUP BY b.full_name, b.state HAVING SUM(l.principal_usd) > (SELECT AVG(principal_usd) FROM loans WHERE borrower_id IN (SELECT id FROM borrowers WHERE state = b.state)) ORDER BY total_principal DESC, b.full_name ASC

question: Identify borrowers at risk based on having missed repayments and card spending above $100.
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT b.full_name, b.risk_tier, b.state FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id JOIN repayments r ON r.loan_id = l.id JOIN cards c ON c.borrower_id = b.id JOIN card_transactions ct ON ct.card_id = c.id WHERE r.repayment_status = 'missed' GROUP BY b.full_name, b.risk_tier, b.state HAVING SUM(ct.amount_usd) > 100 ORDER BY b.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT DISTINCT b.full_name FROM borrowers b JOIN repayments r ON r.borrower_id = b.id JOIN cards c ON c.borrower_id = b.id JOIN card_transactions ct ON ct.card_id = c.id WHERE r.repayment_status = 'missed' AND ct.amount_usd > 100 ORDER BY b.full_name ASC

question: Calculate the net position for each borrower as principal minus total repayments.
gold_sql: SELECT b.full_name, SUM(DISTINCT l.principal_usd) AS total_principal, COALESCE(SUM(r.amount_usd), 0) AS total_repaid, SUM(DISTINCT l.principal_usd) - COALESCE(SUM(r.amount_usd), 0) AS net_position FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id LEFT JOIN repayments r ON r.loan_id = l.id GROUP BY b.full_name ORDER BY net_position DESC, b.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT b.full_name, l.principal_usd - COALESCE(SUM(r.amount_usd), 0) AS net_position FROM borrowers b JOIN loans l ON l.borrower_id = b.id LEFT JOIN repayments r ON r.loan_id = l.id GROUP BY b.full_name, l.principal_usd ORDER BY net_position DESC, b.full_name ASC

question: Calculate the running total of repayments for each loan.
gold_sql: SELECT r.loan_id, r.id AS repayment_id, r.due_date, r.amount_usd, SUM(r.amount_usd) OVER (PARTITION BY r.loan_id ORDER BY r.due_date, r.id) AS running_total FROM repayments r ORDER BY r.loan_id ASC, r.due_date ASC, r.id ASC
pred_sql: SELECT l.id AS loan_id, l.principal_usd, SUM(r.amount_usd) OVER (PARTITION BY r.loan_id ORDER BY r.due_date) AS running_total_repayments FROM loans l LEFT JOIN repayments r ON r.loan_id = l.id ORDER BY l.id, r.due_date

question: Show the month-over-month application count trend.
gold_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', submitted_at) AS month, COUNT(*) AS application_count FROM applications GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', submitted_at) ORDER BY month ASC
pred_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', submitted_at) AS submission_month, COUNT(*) AS application_count FROM applications GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', submitted_at) ORDER BY submission_month ASC

question: Rank borrowers by total card spending within their state.
gold_sql: SELECT b.state, b.full_name, SUM(ct.amount_usd) AS total_spending, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY b.state ORDER BY SUM(ct.amount_usd) DESC) AS spending_rank FROM borrowers b JOIN cards c ON c.borrower_id = b.id JOIN card_transactions ct ON ct.card_id = c.id GROUP BY b.state, b.full_name ORDER BY b.state ASC, spending_rank ASC, b.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT b.full_name, b.state, SUM(ct.amount_usd) AS total_spent_usd, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY b.state ORDER BY SUM(ct.amount_usd) DESC) AS state_rank FROM borrowers b JOIN cards c ON c.borrower_id = b.id JOIN card_transactions ct ON ct.card_id = c.id WHERE ct.transaction_status = 'completed' GROUP BY b.full_name, b.state ORDER BY total_spent_usd DESC, b.full_name ASC

question: Find the first and most recent transaction for each card.
gold_sql: SELECT c.id AS card_id, b.full_name, MIN(ct.transacted_at) AS first_transaction, MAX(ct.transacted_at) AS last_transaction, COUNT(ct.id) AS transaction_count FROM cards c JOIN borrowers b ON c.borrower_id = b.id LEFT JOIN card_transactions ct ON ct.card_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id, b.full_name ORDER BY c.id ASC
pred_sql: SELECT c.id AS card_id, MIN(ct.transacted_at) AS first_transaction_at, MAX(ct.transacted_at) AS last_transaction_at FROM cards c LEFT JOIN card_transactions ct ON ct.card_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id ORDER BY c.id ASC

question: Which patients have never had a lab order?
gold_sql: SELECT pa.full_name FROM patients pa WHERE pa.id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT a.patient_id FROM appointments a JOIN lab_orders lo ON lo.appointment_id = a.id) ORDER BY pa.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT p.full_name FROM patients p WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM lab_orders lo JOIN appointments a ON a.id = lo.appointment_id WHERE a.patient_id = p.id) ORDER BY p.full_name ASC

question: Show the breakdown of appointment statuses by specialty.
gold_sql: SELECT pr.specialty, a.appointment_status, COUNT(a.id) AS appointment_count FROM providers pr JOIN appointments a ON a.provider_id = pr.id GROUP BY pr.specialty, a.appointment_status ORDER BY pr.specialty ASC, appointment_count DESC
pred_sql: SELECT pr.specialty, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.appointment_status = 'completed' THEN 1 END) AS completed, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.appointment_status = 'cancelled' THEN 1 END) AS cancelled FROM providers pr JOIN appointments a ON a.provider_id = pr.id GROUP BY pr.specialty ORDER BY pr.specialty ASC

question: Which providers have the highest average prescription days supply?
gold_sql: SELECT pr.provider_name, AVG(p.days_supply) AS avg_days_supply FROM providers pr JOIN appointments a ON a.provider_id = pr.id JOIN prescriptions p ON p.appointment_id = a.id GROUP BY pr.provider_name ORDER BY avg_days_supply DESC, pr.provider_name ASC LIMIT 5
pred_sql: SELECT pr.provider_name, AVG(ps.days_supply) AS avg_days FROM providers pr JOIN appointments a ON a.provider_id = pr.id JOIN prescriptions ps ON ps.appointment_id = a.id GROUP BY pr.provider_name ORDER BY avg_days DESC, pr.provider_name ASC LIMIT 10

question: Show all completed appointments with their diagnoses and claims.
gold_sql: SELECT a.id AS appointment_id, a.scheduled_at, d.diagnosis_code, cl.billed_amount_usd, cl.claim_status FROM appointments a LEFT JOIN diagnoses d ON d.appointment_id = a.id LEFT JOIN claims cl ON cl.appointment_id = a.id WHERE a.appointment_status = 'completed' ORDER BY a.id ASC
pred_sql: SELECT a.id AS appointment_id, p.full_name, d.diagnosis_code, cl.paid_amount_usd FROM appointments a JOIN patients p ON p.id = a.patient_id LEFT JOIN diagnoses d ON d.appointment_id = a.id LEFT JOIN claims cl ON cl.appointment_id = a.id WHERE a.appointment_status = 'completed' ORDER BY a.id ASC

question: What is the total paid revenue for each diagnosis category?
gold_sql: SELECT d.diagnosis_category, SUM(cl.paid_amount_usd) AS total_paid FROM diagnoses d JOIN appointments a ON a.id = d.appointment_id JOIN claims cl ON cl.appointment_id = a.id WHERE cl.claim_status = 'paid' GROUP BY d.diagnosis_category ORDER BY total_paid DESC, d.diagnosis_category ASC
pred_sql: SELECT d.diagnosis_category, SUM(c.paid_amount_usd) AS total_paid_revenue FROM diagnoses d JOIN appointments a ON a.id = d.appointment_id JOIN claims c ON c.appointment_id = a.id WHERE c.claim_status = 'paid' GROUP BY d.diagnosis_category ORDER BY total_paid_revenue DESC, d.diagnosis_category ASC

question: Show total prescriptions count by provider for active prescriptions only.
gold_sql: SELECT pr.provider_name, COUNT(p.id) AS active_prescription_count FROM providers pr JOIN appointments a ON a.provider_id = pr.id JOIN prescriptions p ON p.appointment_id = a.id WHERE p.prescription_status = 'active' GROUP BY pr.provider_name ORDER BY active_prescription_count DESC, pr.provider_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT pr.provider_name, COUNT(pr.id) AS prescription_count FROM providers pr JOIN appointments a ON a.provider_id = pr.id JOIN prescriptions p ON p.appointment_id = a.id WHERE p.prescription_status = 'active' GROUP BY pr.provider_name ORDER BY prescription_count DESC, pr.provider_name ASC

question: Find clinics where the average claim payment is less than the average claim billed.
gold_sql: SELECT c.clinic_name, AVG(cl.billed_amount_usd) AS avg_billed, AVG(cl.paid_amount_usd) AS avg_paid FROM clinics c JOIN providers pr ON pr.clinic_id = c.id JOIN appointments a ON a.provider_id = pr.id JOIN claims cl ON cl.appointment_id = a.id GROUP BY c.clinic_name HAVING AVG(cl.paid_amount_usd) < AVG(cl.billed_amount_usd) ORDER BY c.clinic_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT c.clinic_name, AVG(cl.paid_amount_usd) AS avg_paid, AVG(cl.billed_amount_usd) AS avg_billed FROM clinics c JOIN providers pr ON pr.clinic_id = c.id JOIN appointments a ON a.provider_id = pr.id JOIN claims cl ON cl.appointment_id = a.id WHERE cl.claim_status = 'paid' GROUP BY c.clinic_name HAVING AVG(cl.paid_amount_usd) < AVG(cl.billed_amount_usd) ORDER BY avg_paid ASC, c.clinic_name ASC

question: Calculate the claim denial rate (non-paid / total) by payer type.
gold_sql: SELECT pa.payer_type, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN cl.claim_status != 'paid' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS denial_rate_pct FROM patients pa JOIN appointments a ON a.patient_id = pa.id JOIN claims cl ON cl.appointment_id = a.id GROUP BY pa.payer_type ORDER BY denial_rate_pct DESC, pa.payer_type ASC
pred_sql: SELECT p.payer_type, ROUND(1.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN c.claim_status <> 'paid' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(c.id), 2) AS denial_rate FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON a.patient_id = p.id LEFT JOIN claims c ON c.appointment_id = a.id GROUP BY p.payer_type ORDER BY denial_rate DESC, p.payer_type ASC

question: Which properties have guests from the most diverse set of countries?
gold_sql: SELECT p.property_name, COUNT(DISTINCT g.home_country) AS unique_countries FROM properties p JOIN rooms r ON r.property_id = p.id JOIN reservations res ON res.room_id = r.id JOIN guests g ON g.id = res.guest_id GROUP BY p.property_name ORDER BY unique_countries DESC, p.property_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT p.property_name, COUNT(DISTINCT g.home_country) AS unique_countries FROM properties p JOIN rooms r ON r.property_id = p.id JOIN reservations res ON res.room_id = r.id JOIN guests g ON g.id = res.guest_id GROUP BY p.property_name ORDER BY unique_countries DESC, p.property_name ASC

question: Which rooms have average nightly rates above the overall average?
gold_sql: SELECT r.room_number, p.property_name, AVG(res.nightly_rate_usd) AS avg_rate FROM rooms r JOIN properties p ON p.id = r.property_id JOIN reservations res ON res.room_id = r.id GROUP BY r.id, r.room_number, p.property_name HAVING AVG(res.nightly_rate_usd) > (SELECT AVG(nightly_rate_usd) FROM reservations) ORDER BY avg_rate DESC, p.property_name ASC, r.room_number ASC
pred_sql: SELECT r.room_number, AVG(res.nightly_rate_usd) AS avg_nightly_rate FROM rooms r JOIN reservations res ON res.room_id = r.id GROUP BY r.room_number HAVING AVG(res.nightly_rate_usd) > (SELECT AVG(nightly_rate_usd) FROM reservations) ORDER BY avg_nightly_rate DESC, r.room_number ASC

question: What is the total ancillary revenue (non-room) per loyalty tier?
gold_sql: SELECT g.loyalty_tier, SUM(fi.item_amount_usd) AS ancillary_revenue FROM guests g JOIN reservations res ON res.guest_id = g.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = res.id JOIN folio_items fi ON fi.folio_id = f.id WHERE fi.item_type != 'room' GROUP BY g.loyalty_tier ORDER BY ancillary_revenue DESC, g.loyalty_tier ASC
pred_sql: SELECT g.loyalty_tier, SUM(fi.item_amount_usd) AS ancillary_revenue FROM guests g JOIN reservations res ON res.guest_id = g.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = res.id JOIN folio_items fi ON fi.folio_id = f.id WHERE fi.item_type != 'room' GROUP BY g.loyalty_tier ORDER BY ancillary_revenue DESC, g.loyalty_tier ASC

question: Find guests who have stayed at multiple properties.
gold_sql: SELECT g.full_name, COUNT(DISTINCT r.property_id) AS property_count FROM guests g JOIN reservations res ON res.guest_id = g.id JOIN rooms r ON r.id = res.room_id GROUP BY g.full_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT r.property_id) > 1 ORDER BY property_count DESC, g.full_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT g.full_name, COUNT(DISTINCT p.property_name) AS property_count FROM guests g JOIN reservations r ON r.guest_id = g.id JOIN rooms rm ON rm.id = r.room_id JOIN properties p ON p.id = rm.property_id GROUP BY g.full_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.property_name) > 1 ORDER BY property_count DESC, g.full_name ASC

question: What is the RevPAR (revenue per available room) by brand tier?
gold_sql: SELECT p.brand_tier, SUM(f.total_amount_usd) / COUNT(DISTINCT r.id) AS revpar FROM properties p JOIN rooms r ON r.property_id = p.id JOIN reservations res ON res.room_id = r.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = res.id GROUP BY p.brand_tier ORDER BY revpar DESC, p.brand_tier ASC
pred_sql: SELECT p.brand_tier, ROUND(SUM(f.total_amount_usd) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT r.room_id), 2) AS revpar FROM properties p JOIN rooms r ON r.property_id = p.id JOIN reservations res ON res.room_id = r.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = res.id WHERE res.reservation_status = 'checked_out' GROUP BY p.brand_tier ORDER BY revpar DESC, p.brand_tier ASC

question: Calculate the percentage of guests in each loyalty tier that stayed at luxury properties.
gold_sql: SELECT g.loyalty_tier, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.brand_tier = 'luxury' THEN g.id END) AS luxury_guests, COUNT(DISTINCT g.id) AS total_guests, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.brand_tier = 'luxury' THEN g.id END) / COUNT(DISTINCT g.id), 2) AS pct_luxury FROM guests g JOIN reservations res ON res.guest_id = g.id JOIN rooms r ON r.id = res.room_id JOIN properties p ON p.id = r.property_id GROUP BY g.loyalty_tier ORDER BY pct_luxury DESC, g.loyalty_tier ASC
pred_sql: SELECT g.loyalty_tier, COUNT(DISTINCT res.guest_id) AS guest_count, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT res.guest_id) / SUM(COUNT(DISTINCT res.guest_id)) OVER (), 2) AS pct_of_total FROM guests g JOIN reservations res ON res.guest_id = g.id JOIN rooms r ON r.id = res.room_id JOIN properties p ON p.id = r.property_id WHERE p.brand_tier = 'luxury' GROUP BY g.loyalty_tier ORDER BY guest_count DESC, g.loyalty_tier ASC

question: Which property generated the highest total revenue?
gold_sql: SELECT p.property_name, SUM(f.total_amount_usd) AS total_revenue FROM properties p JOIN rooms r ON r.property_id = p.id JOIN reservations res ON res.room_id = r.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = res.id GROUP BY p.property_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC, p.property_name ASC LIMIT 1
pred_sql: SELECT p.property_name, SUM(f.total_amount_usd) AS total_revenue FROM properties p JOIN rooms r ON r.property_id = p.id JOIN reservations res ON res.room_id = r.id JOIN folios f ON f.reservation_id = res.id GROUP BY p.property_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC, p.property_name ASC LIMIT 1

question: What is the housekeeping task completion rate by staff member?
gold_sql: SELECT s.staff_name, COUNT(h.id) AS total_tasks, SUM(CASE WHEN h.task_status = 'completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS completed, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN h.task_status = 'completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(h.id), 2) AS completion_rate FROM staff s LEFT JOIN housekeeping_tasks h ON h.assigned_staff_id = s.id GROUP BY s.staff_name ORDER BY completion_rate DESC, s.staff_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT s.staff_name, COUNT(CASE WHEN ht.task_status = 'completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS completion_rate_pct FROM staff s LEFT JOIN housekeeping_tasks ht ON ht.assigned_staff_id = s.id GROUP BY s.staff_name ORDER BY completion_rate_pct DESC, s.staff_name ASC

question: Find the most active hiring month for each department
gold_sql: SELECT department_name, hire_month, hire_count FROM (SELECT d.department_name, strftime('%Y-%m', a.applied_at) AS hire_month, COUNT(a.id) AS hire_count, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY d.id ORDER BY COUNT(a.id) DESC) AS rn FROM departments d JOIN job_openings j ON j.department_id = d.id JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id WHERE a.application_status = 'hired' GROUP BY d.id, d.department_name, strftime('%Y-%m', a.applied_at)) WHERE rn = 1 ORDER BY hire_count DESC, department_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT d.department_name, strftime('%Y-%m', j.opened_at) AS hiring_month, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS hired_count FROM departments d JOIN job_openings j ON j.department_id = d.id JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id WHERE a.application_status = 'hired' GROUP BY d.department_name, strftime('%Y-%m', j.opened_at) ORDER BY d.department_name, hired_count DESC, hiring_month ASC

question: Show job openings with their competitive ratio (applications per opening)
gold_sql: SELECT j.opening_title, j.opening_status, COUNT(a.id) AS application_count, ROUND(1.0 * COUNT(a.id) / (SELECT AVG(cnt) FROM (SELECT COUNT(id) AS cnt FROM applications GROUP BY job_opening_id)), 2) AS competitiveness_ratio FROM job_openings j LEFT JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id GROUP BY j.id, j.opening_title, j.opening_status ORDER BY competitiveness_ratio DESC, j.opening_title ASC
pred_sql: SELECT j.opening_title, COUNT(a.id) AS application_count, ROUND(CAST(COUNT(a.id) AS REAL) / 1.0, 2) AS competitive_ratio FROM job_openings j LEFT JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id GROUP BY j.id, j.opening_title ORDER BY application_count DESC, j.opening_title ASC

question: Rank source channels by their conversion funnel performance
gold_sql: WITH funnel AS (SELECT c.source_channel, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS candidates, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS applications, COUNT(DISTINCT i.application_id) AS interviewed, COUNT(DISTINCT o.application_id) AS offers, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN a.application_status = 'hired' THEN a.id END) AS hires FROM candidates c LEFT JOIN applications a ON a.candidate_id = c.id LEFT JOIN interviews i ON i.application_id = a.id LEFT JOIN offers o ON o.application_id = a.id GROUP BY c.source_channel) SELECT source_channel, candidates, applications, interviewed, offers, hires, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY hires DESC) AS hire_rank FROM funnel ORDER BY hire_rank ASC, source_channel ASC
pred_sql: SELECT c.source_channel, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS total_applications, COUNT(DISTINCT i.id) AS total_interviews, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS total_offers FROM candidates c JOIN applications a ON a.candidate_id = c.id LEFT JOIN interviews i ON i.application_id = a.id LEFT JOIN offers o ON o.application_id = a.id GROUP BY c.source_channel ORDER BY total_offers DESC, total_interviews DESC, total_applications DESC

question: Calculate the variance in offered salaries by department
gold_sql: SELECT d.department_name, COUNT(o.id) AS offer_count, AVG(o.annual_salary_usd) AS avg_salary, ROUND(AVG(o.annual_salary_usd * o.annual_salary_usd) - AVG(o.annual_salary_usd) * AVG(o.annual_salary_usd), 2) AS salary_variance FROM departments d JOIN job_openings j ON j.department_id = d.id JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id JOIN offers o ON o.application_id = a.id GROUP BY d.department_name HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 1 ORDER BY salary_variance DESC, d.department_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT d.department_name, ROUND(SUM(ABS(o.annual_salary_usd - AVG(o.annual_salary_usd)) * 1.0 / COUNT(o.id)), 2) AS salary_variance FROM departments d JOIN job_openings j ON j.department_id = d.id JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id JOIN offers o ON o.application_id = a.id GROUP BY d.department_name ORDER BY salary_variance DESC, d.department_name ASC

question: Build a cohort analysis of candidates by application month and current status
gold_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', a.applied_at) AS cohort_month, a.application_status, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS candidate_count FROM candidates c JOIN applications a ON a.candidate_id = c.id GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', a.applied_at), a.application_status ORDER BY cohort_month ASC, candidate_count DESC
pred_sql: SELECT c.full_name, strftime('%Y-%m', a.applied_at) AS application_month, a.application_status AS current_status FROM candidates c JOIN applications a ON a.candidate_id = c.id ORDER BY application_month DESC, c.full_name ASC

question: Detect interview bottlenecks by calculating average days between interview stages
gold_sql: WITH interview_order AS (SELECT i.application_id, i.interview_stage, i.scheduled_at, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY i.application_id ORDER BY i.scheduled_at) AS stage_order FROM interviews i) SELECT i1.interview_stage AS from_stage, i2.interview_stage AS to_stage, AVG(julianday(i2.scheduled_at) - julianday(i1.scheduled_at)) AS avg_days_between FROM interview_order i1 JOIN interview_order i2 ON i1.application_id = i2.application_id AND i2.stage_order = i1.stage_order + 1 GROUP BY i1.interview_stage, i2.interview_stage ORDER BY avg_days_between DESC
pred_sql: SELECT i.interview_stage, AVG(julianday(i2.scheduled_at) - julianday(i.scheduled_at)) AS avg_days_to_next FROM interviews i LEFT JOIN interviews i2 ON i.application_id = i2.application_id AND i.id < i2.id GROUP BY i.interview_stage ORDER BY avg_days_to_next DESC, i.interview_stage ASC

question: Compute the weighted average salary offer based on department headcount
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(o.annual_salary_usd * dept_weight.headcount) / SUM(dept_weight.headcount) AS weighted_avg_salary FROM offers o JOIN applications a ON o.application_id = a.id JOIN job_openings j ON a.job_opening_id = j.id JOIN (SELECT department_id, COUNT(id) AS headcount FROM employees WHERE employment_status = 'active' GROUP BY department_id) dept_weight ON j.department_id = dept_weight.department_id
pred_sql: SELECT d.department_name, AVG(o.annual_salary_usd * COUNT(e.id) OVER (PARTITION BY e.department_id) / COUNT(e.id) OVER () AS weighted_avg_salary FROM departments d JOIN employees e ON e.department_id = d.id JOIN applications a ON a.candidate_id = e.id JOIN offers o ON o.application_id = a.id WHERE o.offer_status = 'accepted' GROUP BY d.department_name ORDER BY weighted_avg_salary DESC, d.department_name ASC

question: Generate a comprehensive hiring dashboard with KPIs by region
gold_sql: SELECT d.region, COUNT(DISTINCT d.id) AS department_count, COUNT(DISTINCT j.id) AS total_openings, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN j.opening_status = 'open' THEN j.id END) AS open_positions, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS total_applications, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN a.application_status = 'hired' THEN a.id END) AS total_hires, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN a.application_status = 'hired' THEN a.id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT a.id), 0), 2) AS hire_rate_pct, ROUND(AVG(o.annual_salary_usd), 2) AS avg_offer_salary FROM departments d LEFT JOIN job_openings j ON j.department_id = d.id LEFT JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id LEFT JOIN offers o ON o.application_id = a.id GROUP BY d.region ORDER BY total_hires DESC, d.region ASC
pred_sql: SELECT d.region, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS candidates, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS applications, COUNT(DISTINCT i.id) AS interviews, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS offers FROM departments d LEFT JOIN job_openings j ON j.department_id = d.id LEFT JOIN applications a ON a.job_opening_id = j.id LEFT JOIN candidates c ON c.id = a.candidate_id LEFT JOIN interviews i ON i.application_id = a.id LEFT JOIN offers o ON o.application_id = a.id GROUP BY d.region ORDER BY d.region ASC

question: Which paid delivered orders were shipped after payment on a later calendar date and still have no linked return request?
gold_sql: SELECT o.id AS order_id, o.channel, sh.carrier FROM orders o JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid' JOIN shipments sh ON sh.order_id = o.id AND sh.shipment_status = 'delivered' LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id LEFT JOIN returns r ON r.order_item_id = oi.id GROUP BY o.id, o.channel, sh.carrier, date(sh.shipped_at), date(p.paid_at) HAVING COUNT(r.id) = 0 AND date(sh.shipped_at) > date(p.paid_at) ORDER BY order_id ASC, o.channel ASC, sh.carrier ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.id AS order_id, o.ordered_at, p.paid_at, s.shipped_at FROM orders o JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id JOIN shipments s ON s.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'delivered' AND p.payment_status = 'paid' AND s.shipment_status = 'delivered' AND s.shipped_at > p.paid_at AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM returns r JOIN order_items oi ON oi.id = r.order_item_id WHERE r.return_status != 'rejected' AND oi.order_id = o.id) ORDER BY o.id ASC

question: Which sellers generated more paid GMV from web orders than from non-web orders?
gold_sql: SELECT s.seller_name, SUM(CASE WHEN o.channel = 'web' THEN oi.item_amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS web_paid_gmv_usd, SUM(CASE WHEN o.channel <> 'web' THEN oi.item_amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS non_web_paid_gmv_usd FROM sellers s JOIN products pr ON pr.seller_id = s.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = pr.id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid' GROUP BY s.seller_name HAVING web_paid_gmv_usd > non_web_paid_gmv_usd ORDER BY web_paid_gmv_usd DESC, s.seller_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT s.seller_name, SUM(oi.item_amount_usd) AS web_gmv, 0 AS non_web_gmv FROM sellers s JOIN products pr ON pr.seller_id = s.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = pr.id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id WHERE p.payment_status = 'paid' AND o.channel = 'web' GROUP BY s.seller_name UNION ALL SELECT s.seller_name, 0 AS web_gmv, SUM(oi.item_amount_usd) AS non_web_gmv FROM sellers s JOIN products pr ON pr.seller_id = s.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = pr.id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id WHERE p.payment_status = 'paid' AND o.channel != 'web' GROUP BY s.seller_name ORDER BY web_gmv - non_web_gmv DESC, s.seller_name ASC

question: Which paid orders had a linked return requested more than one day after delivery?
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT o.id AS order_id, c.full_name, r.requested_at FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid' JOIN shipments sh ON sh.order_id = o.id AND sh.shipment_status = 'delivered' JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN returns r ON r.order_item_id = oi.id WHERE julianday(r.requested_at) - julianday(sh.delivered_at) > 1 ORDER BY order_id ASC, c.full_name ASC, r.requested_at ASC
pred_sql: SELECT DISTINCT o.id AS order_id FROM orders o JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id JOIN shipments s ON s.order_id = o.id JOIN returns r ON r.order_item_id IN (SELECT id FROM order_items WHERE order_id = o.id) WHERE p.payment_status = 'paid' AND s.shipment_status = 'delivered' AND r.return_status != 'rejected' AND r.requested_at > s.delivered_at + INTERVAL 1 DAY ORDER BY o.id ASC

question: Which carriers have refund dollars per paid delivered order above the overall carrier average?
gold_sql: WITH carrier_metrics AS (SELECT sh.carrier, COALESCE(SUM(r.refund_amount_usd), 0) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS refund_usd_per_paid_delivered_order FROM shipments sh JOIN orders o ON o.id = sh.order_id JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid' LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id LEFT JOIN returns r ON r.order_item_id = oi.id WHERE sh.shipment_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY sh.carrier) SELECT carrier, refund_usd_per_paid_delivered_order FROM carrier_metrics WHERE refund_usd_per_paid_delivered_order > (SELECT AVG(refund_usd_per_paid_delivered_order) FROM carrier_metrics) ORDER BY refund_usd_per_paid_delivered_order DESC, carrier ASC
pred_sql: WITH paid_delivered_orders AS ( SELECT DISTINCT o.id AS order_id FROM orders o JOIN shipments s ON s.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND s.shipment_status = 'delivered' ), carrier_refund_totals AS ( SELECT sh.carrier, SUM(r.refund_amount_usd) AS total_refund_usd FROM paid_delivered_orders pdo JOIN shipments sh ON sh.order_id = pdo.order_id LEFT JOIN returns r ON r.order_item_id IN (SELECT oi.id FROM order_items oi WHERE oi.order_id = pdo.order_id) GROUP BY sh.carrier ) SELECT c.carrier, ROUND(c.total_refund_usd / pd.num_paid_delivered_orders, 2) AS avg_refund_per_paid_delivered_order FROM carrier_refund_totals c JOIN ( SELECT carrier, COUNT(*) AS num_paid_delivered_orders FROM paid_delivered_orders pdo JOIN shipments sh ON sh.order_id = pdo.order_id GROUP BY sh.carrier ) pd ON pd.carrier = c.carrier WHERE ROUND(c.total_refund_usd / pd.num_paid_delivered_orders, 2) > ( SELECT ROUND(AVG(total_refund_usd / num_paid_delivered_orders), 2) FROM carrier_refund_totals crt JOIN ( SELECT carrier, COUNT

question: Which category and channel combinations have a paid order-item return rate above the overall paid order-item return rate?
gold_sql: WITH combo_return_rates AS (SELECT cat.category_name, o.channel, COUNT(r.id) * 1.0 / COUNT(oi.id) AS return_rate FROM categories cat JOIN products pr ON pr.category_id = cat.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = pr.id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid' LEFT JOIN returns r ON r.order_item_id = oi.id GROUP BY cat.category_name, o.channel) SELECT category_name, channel, return_rate FROM combo_return_rates WHERE return_rate > (SELECT COUNT(r.id) * 1.0 / COUNT(oi.id) FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid' LEFT JOIN returns r ON r.order_item_id = oi.id) ORDER BY return_rate DESC, category_name ASC, channel ASC
pred_sql: WITH paid_order_items AS ( SELECT oi.id AS order_item_id, oi.quantity, o.channel FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM payments p WHERE p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid') ), paid_returns AS ( SELECT ri.order_item_id, ri.refund_amount_usd, ri.return_status FROM returns ri JOIN paid_order_items poi ON poi.order_item_id = ri.order_item_id ) SELECT c.category_name, o.channel, COUNT(DISTINCT pr.order_item_id) * 1.0 / SUM(poi.quantity) OVER () AS return_rate FROM paid_order_items poi JOIN products p ON p.id = poi.product_id JOIN categories c ON c.id = p.category_id JOIN orders o ON o.id = poi.order_id LEFT JOIN paid_returns pr ON pr.order_item_id = poi.order_item_id GROUP BY c.category_name, o.channel HAVING return_rate > (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pr.order_item_id) * 1.0 / SUM(poi.quantity) FROM paid_order_items poi LEFT JOIN paid_returns pr ON pr.order_item_id = poi.order_item_id) ORDER BY return_rate DESC, c.category_name ASC, o.channel

question: Which paid orders contain more than one order-item line while all of those lines come from the same seller?
gold_sql: SELECT o.id AS order_id, COUNT(oi.id) AS order_item_lines FROM orders o JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid' JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products pr ON pr.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY o.id HAVING COUNT(oi.id) > 1 AND COUNT(DISTINCT pr.seller_id) = 1 ORDER BY order_item_lines DESC, order_id ASC
pred_sql: SELECT o.id AS order_id, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.product_id) AS unique_product_count FROM orders o JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid' JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products pr ON pr.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY o.id HAVING COUNT(oi.id) > 1 AND COUNT(DISTINCT pr.seller_id) = 1 ORDER BY unique_product_count DESC, order_id ASC

question: Which customer, seller, category, and channel combinations contain more than one paid order-item line?
gold_sql: SELECT c.full_name, s.seller_name, cat.category_name, o.channel, COUNT(oi.id) AS paid_order_item_lines FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id AND p.payment_status = 'paid' JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products pr ON pr.id = oi.product_id JOIN sellers s ON s.id = pr.seller_id JOIN categories cat ON cat.id = pr.category_id GROUP BY c.full_name, s.seller_name, cat.category_name, o.channel HAVING COUNT(oi.id) > 1 ORDER BY paid_order_item_lines DESC, c.full_name ASC, s.seller_name ASC, cat.category_name ASC, o.channel ASC
pred_sql: SELECT c.full_name AS customer, s.seller_name, cat.category_name, o.channel, COUNT(oi.id) AS paid_order_items FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN sellers s ON s.id = p.seller_id JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id JOIN categories cat ON cat.id = p.category_id JOIN payments pay ON pay.order_id = o.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE pay.payment_status = 'paid' GROUP BY c.full_name, s.seller_name, cat.category_name, o.channel HAVING COUNT(oi.id) > 1 ORDER BY paid_order_items DESC, c.full_name ASC, s.seller_name ASC, cat.category_name ASC, o.channel ASC

question: Which customer-seller pairs appear in every delivered web order placed by that customer?
gold_sql: SELECT c.full_name, s.seller_name FROM customers c JOIN sellers s WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.customer_id = c.id AND o.status = 'delivered' AND o.channel = 'web') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.customer_id = c.id AND o.status = 'delivered' AND o.channel = 'web' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM order_items oi JOIN products pr ON pr.id = oi.product_id WHERE oi.order_id = o.id AND pr.seller_id = s.id)) ORDER BY c.full_name ASC, s.seller_name ASC
pred_sql: SELECT c.full_name AS customer, s.seller_name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products pr ON pr.id = oi.product_id JOIN sellers s ON s.id = pr.seller_id WHERE o.status = 'delivered' AND o.channel = 'web' GROUP BY c.full_name, s.seller_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT o2.id) FROM orders o2 WHERE o2.customer_id = c.id AND o2.status = 'delivered' AND o2.channel = 'web') ORDER BY order_count DESC, c.full_name ASC, s.seller_name ASC

question: Hard: clients whose total retail spend exceeds 20 and who had completed appointments.
gold_sql: SELECT c.client_id, c.email, SUM(rs.quantity * rs.unit_price) AS retail_spend FROM clients c JOIN retail_sales rs ON rs.client_id = c.client_id WHERE c.client_id IN (SELECT a.client_id FROM appointments a WHERE a.status = 'completed') GROUP BY c.client_id, c.email HAVING SUM(rs.quantity * rs.unit_price) > 20 ORDER BY retail_spend DESC, c.client_id
pred_sql: SELECT c.client_id, c.email, SUM(rs.quantity * rs.unit_price) AS total_spend FROM clients c JOIN retail_sales rs ON rs.client_id = c.client_id JOIN appointments a ON a.client_id = c.client_id WHERE a.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.client_id, c.email HAVING total_spend > 20 ORDER BY total_spend DESC, c.client_id

pred_sql: SELECT t.therapist_id, t.first_name, t.last_name FROM therapists t WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM appointments a WHERE a.therapist_id = t.therapist_id AND a.status = 'completed' AND a.scheduled_at >= '2024-04-04' AND a.scheduled_at <= '2024-06-08') ORDER BY t.therapist_id

question: Hard: routes over 40 km that have no incidents in range 2024-03-27 to 2024-07-06.

question: Hard: lines that had downtime but no quality checks in period 2024-06-06 to 2024-11-15.
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT pl.line_id FROM production_lines pl JOIN downtime_events d ON d.line_id = pl.line_id LEFT JOIN work_orders wo ON wo.line_id = pl.line_id LEFT JOIN lots l ON l.work_order_id = wo.work_order_id LEFT JOIN quality_checks qc ON qc.lot_id = l.lot_id AND qc.check_time >= '2024-06-06' AND qc.check_time <= '2024-11-15' WHERE qc.check_id IS NULL ORDER BY pl.line_id
pred_sql: SELECT DISTINCT pl.line_id FROM production_lines pl JOIN work_orders wo ON wo.line_id = pl.line_id LEFT JOIN lots l ON l.work_order_id = wo.work_order_id LEFT JOIN quality_checks qc ON qc.lot_id = l.lot_id AND qc.check_time >= '2024-06-06' AND qc.check_time <= '2024-11-15' JOIN downtime_events d ON d.line_id = pl.line_id WHERE qc.check_id IS NULL ORDER BY pl.line_id

question: Medium: suppliers by average delivered quantity after 2024-10-10.
gold_sql: SELECT s.supplier_id, AVG(sh.delivered_quantity) AS avg_delivered FROM suppliers s JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id JOIN shipments sh ON sh.po_id = po.po_id WHERE sh.ship_date >= '2024-10-10' GROUP BY s.supplier_id ORDER BY avg_delivered DESC, s.supplier_id
pred_sql: SELECT s.supplier_id, AVG(sh.delivered_quantity) AS avg_delivered FROM suppliers s JOIN purchase_orders po ON po.supplier_id = s.supplier_id JOIN shipments sh ON sh.po_id = po.po_id WHERE sh.ship_date >= '2024-10-10' GROUP BY s.supplier_id ORDER BY avg_delivered DESC, s.supplier_id

question: Hard: purchase orders not fully received by 2024-10-26.
gold_sql: SELECT po.po_id, po.quantity_ordered, COALESCE(SUM(sh.delivered_quantity),0) AS delivered_qty FROM purchase_orders po LEFT JOIN shipments sh ON sh.po_id = po.po_id AND sh.received_date <= '2024-10-26' GROUP BY po.po_id, po.quantity_ordered HAVING COALESCE(SUM(sh.delivered_quantity),0) < po.quantity_ordered ORDER BY po.po_id
pred_sql: SELECT po.po_id, po.quantity_ordered, COALESCE(SUM(sh.delivered_quantity), 0) AS delivered FROM purchase_orders po LEFT JOIN shipments sh ON sh.po_id = po.po_id AND sh.received_date <= '2024-10-26' GROUP BY po.po_id, po.quantity_ordered HAVING COALESCE(SUM(sh.delivered_quantity), 0) < po.quantity_ordered ORDER BY po.po_id

question: Hard: lots where defect rate average exceeds 1.
gold_sql: SELECT l.lot_id, AVG(qc.defect_rate) AS avg_defect_rate FROM lots l JOIN quality_checks qc ON qc.lot_id = l.lot_id GROUP BY l.lot_id HAVING AVG(qc.defect_rate) > 1 ORDER BY avg_defect_rate DESC, l.lot_id
pred_sql: SELECT l.lot_id, AVG(qc.defect_rate) AS avg_defect FROM lots l JOIN quality_checks qc ON qc.lot_id = l.lot_id GROUP BY l.lot_id HAVING AVG(qc.defect_rate) > 1 ORDER BY avg_defect DESC, l.lot_id

question: Medium: work orders scheduled between 2024-01-25 and 2024-06-06.
gold_sql: SELECT work_order_id, line_id, product_family, planned_start, planned_end FROM work_orders WHERE planned_start >= '2024-01-25' AND planned_end <= '2024-06-06' ORDER BY planned_start, work_order_id
pred_sql: SELECT work_order_id, line_id, product_family, planned_start, planned_end FROM work_orders WHERE planned_start >= '2024-01-25' AND planned_end <= '2024-06-06' ORDER BY planned_start, work_order_id

question: Calculate revenue per location from completed orders.
gold_sql: SELECT location_id, SUM(subtotal + tax) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE order_status = 'completed' GROUP BY location_id ORDER BY revenue DESC, location_id
pred_sql: SELECT l.location_name, SUM(o.total) as total_revenue FROM locations l JOIN orders o ON o.location_id = l.location_id WHERE o.order_status = 'completed' GROUP BY l.location_id, l.location_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC, l.location_name

question: Show regions with total seating capacity.
gold_sql: SELECT l.region_id, r.region_name, SUM(l.seating_capacity) AS total_seats FROM locations l JOIN regions r ON r.region_id = l.region_id WHERE l.status = 'active' GROUP BY l.region_id, r.region_name ORDER BY total_seats DESC, l.region_id
pred_sql: SELECT r.region_name, SUM(l.seating_capacity) as total_seats FROM regions r JOIN locations l ON r.region_id = l.region_id GROUP BY r.region_id, r.region_name ORDER BY total_seats DESC, r.region_name

question: Show tenants never late on rent

question: Categories with most items and total revenue
gold_sql: SELECT c.category_name, COUNT(i.item_id) as item_count, COALESCE(SUM(t.amount), 0) as total_revenue FROM photography_studio_categories c LEFT JOIN photography_studio_items i ON c.category_name = i.category LEFT JOIN photography_studio_transactions t ON i.item_id = t.item_id GROUP BY c.category_id, c.category_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
pred_sql: SELECT c.category_name, COUNT(i.item_id) as item_count, SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue FROM photography_studio_categories c JOIN photography_studio_items i ON c.category_id = i.category_id LEFT JOIN photography_studio_transactions t ON i.item_id = t.item_id GROUP BY c.category_id, c.category_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC, item_count DESC, c.category_name ASC

question: Top rated items in each category
gold_sql: SELECT i.category, i.name, AVG(r.rating) as avg_rating FROM photography_studio_items i JOIN photography_studio_reviews r ON i.item_id = r.item_id GROUP BY i.item_id, i.category, i.name ORDER BY i.category, avg_rating DESC
pred_sql: SELECT c.category_name, i.name AS item_name, MAX(r.rating) AS top_rating FROM photography_studio_items i JOIN photography_studio_reviews r ON r.item_id = i.item_id JOIN photography_studio_categories c ON c.category_id = i.category JOIN photography_studio_categories p ON p.category_id = c.parent_category GROUP BY c.category_name, i.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT r.review_id) >= 3 ORDER BY c.category_name ASC, top_rating DESC, i.name ASC

question: Owners with unpaid invoices and active reservations
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT o.first_name, o.last_name, o.email FROM owners o JOIN pets p ON o.owner_id = p.owner_id JOIN reservations r ON p.pet_id = r.pet_id JOIN invoices i ON r.reservation_id = i.reservation_id WHERE i.status = 'unpaid' AND r.status = 'active' ORDER BY o.last_name, o.first_name
